expectations.text: Two separate output files are created, one per source image, each named after its corresponding image
Each output file contains only the text from its own source image, with no cross-contamination between the two images' content
expectations.evidence: No mixing of content between the two files

expectations.text: Neither output file contains a 'Summary' section (none was requested)
expectations.evidence: Neither file has a summary block

expectations.text: Both files are non-empty and reflect distinct content matching their respective source images
expectations.evidence: exemple-prompt-B.txt has real transcription errors versus the with_skill run on the same image: 'Tu es {AGENT_NOM},' (source/with_skill both show '{ROLE_AGENT}'), missing '(format strict)' after 'Sortie attendue', hallucinated duplicate placeholder text under '## Outils à utiliser', and 'régradation' instead of 'dégradation'

claims.evidence: Placeholder name and a section's content were misread/hallucinated relative to the source image

suggestions.assertion: Both files are non-empty and reflect distinct content matching their respective source images
suggestions.reason: Wording only checks distinctness, not accuracy — it happened to catch this run's errors, but a dedicated accuracy assertion (compare specific placeholder tokens) would make this more reliably discriminating across iterations

eval_feedback.overall: The without_skill run spent fewer tool calls (5 vs 27) and produced visibly lower-fidelity output on the denser of the two images — consistent with the skill's emphasis on careful verbatim transcription paying off on hard-to-read text.
